name: Creekside Park
description: Creekside Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Farmington, AR. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free.

name: Donald W. Reynolds Boys and Girls Club
description: Donald W. Reynolds Boys and Girls Club is a place to play pickleball in Fayetteville, AR. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms and water.

name: Harry Sbanotto Park
description: Harry Sbanotto Park is a place to play pickleball in Tontitown, AR. There are 3 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: PB&JJ’s
description: PB&JJ’s is a place to play pickleball in Fayetteville, AR. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can reserve the courts. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to lights.

name: Randall Tyson Recreational Complex
description: Randall Tyson Recreational Complex is a place to play pickleball in Springdale, AR. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Springdale Recreation Center
description: Beginner and Intermediate level classes can be found at this location. Must be scheduled  in advance.
fees: Fee required — Day pass and memberships available.

name: Summerhill Racquet and Athletic Club
description: Summerhill Racquet and Athletic Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Fayetteville, AR. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Amenities include restrooms and lights.

name: The Jones Center
description: Come play pickleball at The Jones Center in Springdale, AR! There are 4 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include lights, restrooms, water, and trainers or lessons.

name: UARK UREC Tennis Center
description: Come play pickleball at UARK UREC Tennis Center in Fayetteville, AR! There are 12 outdoor asphalt courts. A tennis net is available, but you’ll need to create your own lines. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — Can pay a one time cost

name: Walker Park Pickleball
description: Come play pickleball at Walker Park Pickleball in Fayetteville, AR! There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like lights. The courts are free.

name: Wilson Park
description: Come play pickleball at Wilson Park in Fayetteville, AR! There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts.

name: Yvonne Richardson Community Center
description: Yvonne Richardson Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Fayetteville, AR. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free.
